summary: Tests are built around speculative API assumptions (__testables exports with guessed function names) that have no grounding in the actual repository. The plan is extremely vague and does not specify any concrete module, function, or API—yet the tests invent a detailed contract (formatPlanningBanner, applyPlanningState, getPlanningState, clearPlanningState) with no evidence these exist or will exist. Several major issues make the suite non-executable and unreliable.

findings.title: Import from non-existent module with speculative __testables export
findings.details: The tests import `planFeatureExtension` and `{ __testables }` from `../../extensions/plan-feature.ts`. There is no evidence this module exists in the repository. The plan references `__tests__/extensions/currently-it-is-not-possible-for-me-to-work-easily-on-the-sa.plan.test.ts` as the only relevant file, not `extensions/plan-feature.ts`. The tests will fail at import time with MODULE_NOT_FOUND, not with meaningful RED test failures that guide implementation.
findings.fix: Examine the actual repository structure to identify the real module that renders the planning warning. If none exists yet, the test file should clearly document this and use a conditional/dynamic import or mock that fails gracefully with a clear message, rather than a hard import that crashes the entire suite.
findings.requirement: The plan cites affected modules or explicitly notes when no prior module exists.

findings.title: Guessing function names with ?? fallback chain is not TDD
findings.details: Every test does `__testables.formatPlanningBanner ?? __testables.renderPlanningWarning ?? __testables.formatActivePlanningMessage ?? __testables.getPlanningBanner`. This is speculative API discovery, not test-driven development. TDD tests should define a clear contract that the implementation must satisfy. If the function name is unknown, the test author should pick one and the implementation follows. The fallback chain means the tests don't actually define anything.
findings.fix: Choose a single, definitive function name for the banner formatter (e.g., `formatPlanningBanner`) and use only that name. The implementation must then match this contract. Remove the fallback chain entirely.

findings.title: Plan is too vague to derive meaningful test requirements
findings.details: The plan is essentially a copy-paste of the user request with no architectural decisions, no module identification, and no API design. The acceptance criteria are meta-level ('documented in repo-grounded terms') rather than behavioral. The tests had to invent an entire API surface (formatPlanningBanner, applyPlanningState, getPlanningState, clearPlanningState) with no plan backing. This means the tests are not grounded in the plan at all.
findings.fix: The plan needs to be refined to specify: (1) which source file contains or will contain the banner rendering logic, (2) the function signature for the banner formatter, (3) where in the CLI lifecycle the banner is displayed. Until the plan is concrete, tests should be flagged as speculative.
findings.requirement: The feature behavior is documented in repo-grounded terms.

findings.title: Plan scope says to review existing test file but tests ignore it entirely
findings.details: The plan's scope section says 'Review and update __tests__/extensions/currently-it-is-not-possible-for-me-to-work-easily-on-the-sa.plan.test.ts'. The generated tests create an entirely new file and make no reference to the existing test file's patterns, imports, or structure. The existing file likely reveals the actual module structure and API conventions.
findings.fix: Read the existing test file `__tests__/extensions/currently-it-is-not-possible-for-me-to-work-easily-on-the-sa.plan.test.ts` to understand the real module structure, import paths, and testing patterns. Base the new tests on those actual conventions.

findings.title: applyPlanningState/getPlanningState/clearPlanningState tests have no plan backing
findings.details: The last two tests cover `applyPlanningState`, `getPlanningState`, and `clearPlanningState` — functions that appear nowhere in the plan. The plan only discusses making the warning more visible with colors/borders. These tests appear to be invented requirements with no basis in the plan or known codebase.
findings.fix: Remove tests for applyPlanningState/getPlanningState/clearPlanningState unless the plan is updated to include state management requirements, or unless evidence from the existing codebase shows these functions exist and need testing.
findings.requirement: Acceptance criteria cover the primary user-visible flow and at least one edge case.

findings.title: Missing major behavioral requirement
findings.details: No generated coverage entry clearly maps to: Review and update __tests__/extensions/currently-it-is-not-possible-for-me-to-work-easily-on-the-sa.plan.test.ts
findings.fix: Add an explicit behavioral test for this requirement or explain why it cannot be tested realistically.

findings.title: Missing major behavioral requirement
findings.details: No generated coverage entry clearly maps to: Unconfirmed product changes beyond the requested feature
findings.fix: Add an explicit behavioral test for this requirement or explain why it cannot be tested realistically.
findings.requirement: Unconfirmed product changes beyond the requested feature

findings.title: Missing major behavioral requirement
findings.details: No generated coverage entry clearly maps to: (none)
findings.fix: Add an explicit behavioral test for this requirement or explain why it cannot be tested realistically.

findings.title: Missing major behavioral requirement
findings.details: No generated coverage entry clearly maps to: The feature behavior is documented in repo-grounded terms.
findings.fix: Add an explicit behavioral test for this requirement or explain why it cannot be tested realistically.
findings.requirement: The feature behavior is documented in repo-grounded terms.

strengths: Good use of helper functions for ANSI code detection and border character detection
Tests use the repository's existing node:test + node:assert/strict convention
Edge cases for empty title and inactive session are well-conceived
The ANSI detection helpers are thorough, covering multiple SGR code variants
